trade.summary: Corea del Sud-Polonia trade has grown substantially, bolstered by major Corea del Sudn defense and industrial investments. Corea del Sud exports electronics (Samsung, LG operate Polish factories), automobiles, and defense equipment to Polonia. Polonia exports machinery components, food products, and chemicals to Corea del Sud. Both benefit from the EU-Corea del Sud FTA (2011).
top_products_a_to_b: consumer electronics (Samsung, LG factories in Poland)

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Polonia relations have strengthened dramatically since 2022, driven by Polonia's massive defense procurement program (responding to Russia's Ucraina invasion) and Corea del Sud's emergence as a top-tier defense exporter. Polonia signed the largest-ever Corea del Sudn arms deal — worth tens of billions — including K2 tanks, K9 howitzers, FA-50 aircraft, and Chunmoo rocket artillery. This defense partnership has elevated bilateral relations to strategic partnership level.
recent_incidents: Polonia-Corea del Sud mega defense procurement deal (2022-2023): K2 tanks, K9 howitzers, FA-50 jets, Chunmoo MLRS
Growing strategic dialogue following defense partnership elevation

military.summary: The Polonia-Corea del Sud defense relationship is one of Corea del Sud's most significant bilateral defense partnerships. Polonia has contracted to purchase 1,000+ K2 tanks, 672 K9 howitzers, 48 FA-50 aircraft, and 288 Chunmoo MLRS units — the largest arms deal in Corea del Sudn history. Polonia is undertaking licensed production of some systems domestically. Both countries view Russian aggression as an existential threat (Polonia directly, Corea del Sud analogously through North Corea del Sud). Corea del Sud-Polonia share a unique 'island' defense mentality.
military.alliance_status: Strategic defense partners — Poland NATO, Korea US ally

financial.summary: Corea del Sudn FDI in Polonia has grown significantly, led by Samsung (electronics factory in Wronki), LG (appliance manufacturing), and defense industry investments. The massive defense contracts create long-term financial flows and technology transfer commitments. Polish government bonds and equities have limited Corea del Sudn institutional exposure.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Corea del Sud is exploring participation in Polonia's nuclear energy expansion. Both countries are working on energy diversification and security. Some cooperation on LNG supply chain given Polonia's new LNG terminal.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation particularly in defense technology, nuclear energy, and electronics. Defense industrial cooperation under the K2/K9 deals involves significant technology transfer to Polish defense firms. Academic exchanges are expanding, particularly in engineering.
